objective: Review-only QA audit for docs/CI readiness claims covering policy enforcement, depth guard, bounded spawning, telemetry, and recovery semantics.
consensus: Current CI/lint/smoke suite validates structure and documentation cues but not runtime enforcement for premium/banned model policies.
Depth Guard and bounded spawning are primarily phrase/contract checked; runtime degradation, stop/recovery, and pulse-live behavior are under-tested.
Docs alignment is good for install paths and artifact locations, but critical metaswarm policy/recovery details are incomplete in top-level docs.
conflicts_or_variance: Confidence variance (0.58-0.97) reflects differing evidence depth between script-level checks and prose docs.
One worker marked partial due documentation completeness gap despite successful technical inspection.

main_findings.summary: CI workflow lacks explicit metaswarm runtime readiness checks (policy enforcement, telemetry pulse, recovery).
Smoke test asserts structural contracts but misses runtime reject/recovery/partial-path verification.
Depth lint enforces leaf/depth wording but not strict runtime denylist behavior.
README/AGENTS under-document banned-model enforcement, Depth Guard details, and Agent Pulse semantics.

blockers: Task-tool executions returned inline results without a platform background agent id; launch_started used synthetic sync agent ids for telemetry continuity.
